item.title: Mineral Resources Restarts Australia Lithium Mine as Prices Rise
item.description: Mineral Resources Ltd. will restart its Bald Hill lithium mine in Western Australia after an 18-month hiatus, betting that a rebound in prices of the battery material will support renewed production.

item.title: StanChart to Absorb Zodia Subsidiary’s Crypto Custody Business
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc plans to acquire the crypto custody business of its majority-owned subsidiary Zodia Custody Ltd., expanding the reach of the bank’s own digital-asset operations.

item.title: Korea’s Homeplus, Meritz Tussle Over $67 Million Loan Terms
item.description: South Korean retailer Homeplus Co. is objecting to a condition tied to a roughly 100 billion won ($67 million) bridge loan from its largest creditor Meritz Financial Group, placing the emergency funding at risk.

item.title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Warns Stock Rally at Risk From Bond Rout
item.description: Equities are at risk of a significant pullback as the global bond selloff threatens to derail the artificial intelligence-driven rally, according to Morgan Stanley strategists.

item.title: UK Bonds Steady, With 10-Year Yield Holding Near Multi-Year High
item.description: UK bonds edged higher on Monday, though yields remained near multi-year highs as investors weighed the fiscal implications of Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham potentially challenging for UK Prime Minister.

item.title: Kalshi, Polymarket Defy India Ban on Online Betting Platforms
item.description: Polymarket and Kalshi Inc. have been allowing customers in India to sign up and trade on their prediction markets even after the country’s technology ministry warned that the platforms are illegal.

item.title: Kioxia’s Booming Shares Head for Liquidity Boost With US Float
item.description: Kioxia Holdings Corp.’s plan to float American Depositary Shares is setting one of the world’s hottest stocks up for a liquidity boost, tapping into the global fervor over memory chips to expand its investor base.

item.title: Elon Musk Says He Wants to Get SpaceX IPO Underway ‘Pretty Soon’
item.description: Elon Musk said he’s back in Texas working on plans for an initial public offering of SpaceX as the rocket and artificial intelligence company gears up for the largest listing of all time.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Asks EU for Budget Flexibility Over Energy Costs
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni asked the European Commission to extend greater latitude within European Union budget rules to measures aimed at tackling rising energy costs.

item.title: Why the Price of Oil, Beef, Electricity, Everything Else Makes No Sense
item.description: A conversation with Javier Blas and Lorcan Roche Kelly on one price shock after another.

item.title: Odd Lots: Why the Price of Oil and Beef Makes No Sense (Podcast)
item.description: Whether it’s the price of a barrel of Brent crude or a pound of beef, it’s clear prices are skyrocketing for all kinds of goods and commodities. Price shocks and shortages are, if anything, the way consumers understand the economy right now — at the grocery store or at the gas pump. Certainly, current (and future) shocks can be explained by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. But the environment is weirder than just across the board price increases: The price of corn has barely moved, for insta

item.title: India Can Ride Out Disruption After Russian Oil Waiver Lapses
item.description: India’s oil refiners are expected to weather disruption in supply after the US allowed its waiver on Russian crude purchases to expire, thanks to weaker demand and available barrels from the US and the Middle East.

item.title: Billionaire Qatari Family Bids for Ethiopian Airport, Congo Road
item.description: Power International Holding, owned by the Qatari billionaire Al-Khayyat family, is going after mega African infrastructure deals to help expand its footprint on the continent.

item.title: Mouse Plague Drives Australia to Allow Potent Bait to Save Crops
item.description: Australia issued an emergency permit for the use of a powerful poison to halt a swelling mice infestation across several growing regions, as farmers grapple with the fallout from the Iran war and a looming El Niño.

item.title: India Weighs Options to Revive Stalled IDBI Bank Privatization
item.description: Indian authorities are exploring ways to revive interest from potential buyers after their long-running efforts to divest a majority stake in the $8 billion-valued IDBI Bank Ltd. hit a snag, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Switzerland Grows More Than Expected Despite Franc, Energy Spike
item.description: Switzerland’s economy grew faster than anticipated in the first quarter, weathering a spike in energy prices and a strengthening franc that each took effect at the outbreak of the Iran war.

item.title: PolyPeptide Is Said to Shortlist EQT, IDG for Next Bidding Round
item.description: PolyPeptide Group AG has shortlisted private equity firms EQT AB and IDG Capital in the next round of bidding for the Swiss contract drugmaker, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: France’s Lescure Sees Economy Bouncing Back in Second Quarter
item.description: The French economy should return to growth in the second quarter and avoid a recession this year, Finance Minister Roland Lescure said.

item.title: Trump Filing Reveals Stake in Sushi Chain Alongside Big Tech
item.description: Among President Donald Trump’s trades in the first quarter were stock market behemoths including Nvidia Corp., Amazon.com Inc. and Apple Inc. He also took a stake in a $600 million conveyor belt sushi chain.

item.title: Anglo to Sell Australia Coking Coal Mines for $3.88 Billion
item.description: Anglo American Plc agreed to sell its steelmaking coal mines in Australia to Dhilmar Ltd. for as much as $3.88 billion in cash, as the mining company continues to reshape its business.

item.title: Bond Ructions Gatecrash Talks by G-7 on Skewed World Economy
item.description: The global bond selloff will take center stage as Group of Seven finance chiefs discuss how oil-fueled inflation risks overshadowing world economic prospects.

item.title: FTSE 100 and Gilts Fall on Worries Over Inflation, Politics
Helios Towers Bets on Africa’s Data Boom with Congo Investment
item.description: Helios Towers Plc is committing $110 million of new investment in Democratic Republic of Congo, which it considers one of the most attractive growth opportunities across its nine markets.

item.title: JGB Yields Outpace Topix Dividend Yield by Most Since 2007
item.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond yield has risen well above the country’s dividend yields, raising prospects of rotation out from stocks into bonds once volatility in the bond market subsides.

item.title: PG&E Cuts Power to Some Customers in California on Fire Risk
item.description: PG&E Corp. cut power to some customers in California due to dry and windy conditions that have elevated the risk of wildfires.

item.title: British Takeovers Up 250% as Global Buyers Bypass Political Flux
item.description: A British dealmaking boom has London’s M&A advisers on track for their best year in more than a decade, standing in contrast to investor fears over the country’s latest political crisis.

item.title: Absa Weighs Joining Yuan Platform on Thriving China-Africa Trade
item.description: Absa Group Ltd. is weighing joining a payments platform that facilitates direct settlement in Chinese currency, to take advantage of growing trade with Africa’s biggest trading partner.

item.title: Japan’s Five-Year Bond Sale Draws Weaker Demand Amid Global Rout
item.description: Japan’s five-year government bond auction Monday saw lower demand than the 12-month average, as elevated oil prices fueled inflation concerns and drove a global bond rout.

item.title: Hedge Funds Ramp Up Bearish Sterling Bets on Andy Burnham Risk
item.description: Hedge funds and asset managers piled into bearish sterling option bets last week after Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham secured a pathway to potentially challenge for UK prime minister, raising fears of political instability and looser fiscal policy.

item.title: Goldman Says Central Banks to Step Up Gold-Buying, Aiding Prices
item.description: Central banks are expected to step up gold-buying, helping prices to recover by year-end, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
